questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third word in the same way as the second word is related to the first word. ( The words must be considered as meaningful English words and must not be related to each other based on the number of letters/numbers of consonants/vowels in the word) Tachometer : Revolutions per minute :: Anemometer : ?
questions.explanation: A tachometer measures RPM. An anemometer measures wind speed. Thus, Wind speed completes the analogy.

questions.question: Find the missing number: 4 : 76 :: 7 : ?
questions.explanation: Let n be the number. Required number = n3 + 3n For n = 4 = 43 + 3 x 4 = 64 + 12 = 76 For n = 7 = 73 + 3 x 7 = 343 + 21 = 364

questions.question: The question given below consists of a statement, followed by two arguments numbered I and II. You have to decide which of the arguments is a 'strong' argument and which is a 'weak' argument. Statement: Should the use of loudspeakers be banned in residential areas after 10 PM with strict fines? Argument I: Yes, it can protect sleep and health of residents, especially students, elderly people, and patients. Argument II: No, instead of a blanket ban, a permit system with clear decibel limits and emergency exceptions can balance public needs and residents' comfort.
options: Both arguments I and II are strong
Neither argument I nor II is strong
questions.explanation: Argument I is relevant and gives a strong reason based on public well-being, which is central to the time restriction. Argument II is also relevant because it challenges the "ban" part logically and offers a workable alternative that still addresses noise control. So, both arguments are strong.

questions.question: Choose the address that is exactly the same as the one given below. Flat No. 408, B-Wing, Sapphire Residency, Chakala, Andheri (E), Mumbai, Maharashtra - 400099
options: Flat No. 408, B-Wing, Sapphire Residency, Chakala, Andheri (W), Mumbai, Maharashtra - 400099
Flat No. 408, B Wing, Sapphire Residency, Chakala, Andheri (E), Mumbai, Maharashtra - 400099
Flat No. 408, B-Wing, Sapphire Residency, Chakla, Andheri (E), Mumbai, Maharashtra - 400099
Flat No. 408, B-Wing, Sapphire Residency, Chakala, Andheri (E), Mumbai, Maharashtra - 400099
questions.explanation: Option d, is exactly identical to the given address in every character, including "B-Wing" and "(E)". Option a, changes (E) to (W). Option b, removes the hyphen in "B-Wing". Option c, changes "Chakala" to "Chakla".

questions.question: Which of the following belongs to Group S? Group S: 185P240, 195P255, 205P270

Study the following information and answer the given question: M@N means M is the wife of N. M$N means M is the son of N. M#N means M is the daughter of N. Then which of the following relations is true to imply that X is the grand - daughter of B?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30780/image004.png) It is given that, M@N means M is the wife of N. M$N means M is the son of N. M#N means M is the daughter of N. Option a: C@A$D@X#B => C is the wife of A. A is the son of D. D is the wife of X. X is the daughter of B. Here, it is given that 'X is the daughter of B.' So, option 'a' is incorrect. Option b: X$A#C@D$B X is the son of A. A is the daughter of C. C is the wife of D. D is the son of B. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30780/image005.png) Here, X is great grandson of B. So, option 'b' is incorrect. Option c: B@D$X@A$C B is the wife of D. D is the son of X. X is the wife of A. A is the son of C. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30780/image006.png) Here, X is the mother-in-law of B. So, option 'c' is incorrect. Option d: X#C@A$B@D X is the daughter of C. C is the wife of A. A is the son of B. B is the wife of D. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30780/image007.png) Here, X is the grand - daughter of B. So, option 'd' is correct.

questions.question: Instructions: For the question below, carefully examine the Reference Statement. Then, identify the single option among the four alternatives (a, b, c, d) that is an Exact and Complete Replication of the Reference Statement. Pay exceptionally close attention to every character, number, symbol, space and punctuation mark. Reference: Copenhagen; 27 June. 1961.
questions.explanation: Option a: Comma (,) instead of Semicolon (;) Option b: Copenhegen instead of Copenhagen. Option c: Jun instead of June. Option d: It is same as the reference. Hence, option 'd' is correct.

questions.question: In each question below is given a statement followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. You have to assume everything in the statement to be true and on the basis of the information given in the statement, decide which of the suggested courses of action logically follow(s) for pursuing. Statement: Many people complain of health issues after using mustard oil bought loose from the market, and preliminary checks suggest adulteration in several samples collected from multiple vendors. Course of action I: Conduct regular sampling inspections and impose strict penalties, including licence cancellation, on vendors found repeatedly adulterating oil. Course of action II: Ban the sale of all edible oils in the market for six months to ensure safety.
options: Only course of action I follows.
Both course of action I and II follow.
Only course of action II follows.
Neither course of action I nor II follows.
questions.explanation: Course of action I is logical because it targets offenders, improves compliance, and directly reduces adulteration risk. Course of action II is too broad and disrupts essential supply even for safe and compliant sellers. Therefore, only course of action I follows.

questions.question: In this type of questions, question is provided with some issues/disputes/problem, followed by few methods to resolve the problem in statement choosing the appropriate problem solving method that can be implemented. A college lab instructor notices many students submitting practical records with the same observations and graphs, even though readings should differ slightly for each experiment run. The goal is to prevent record copying in future submissions while still supporting weaker students. What is the best preventive measure?
options: Accept all records to maintain peace
Give unique data and verify orally
questions.explanation: Copying reduces when each student must work on a slightly different dataset or readings. Oral verification checks whether the student understands how the observations and graph were made. This prevents copying while still allowing genuine students to learn and improve

questions.question: Find the missing term (?) in the following series. 12, 35, 104, 311, 932, ?
questions.explanation: Logic for the above series is: 12 * 3 - 1 = 35 35 * 3 - 1 = 104 104 * 3 - 1 = 311 311 * 3 - 1 = 932 932 * 3 - 1 = 2795 Hence, the missing term is 2795.

questions.question: Find the missing term (?) in the following series. 49, 170, 339, 628, 989, ?

options: G H E H J B J L B
G L B G V B J L B
G H V H G B J L B
G H V H E B J L B
questions.explanation: The complete series is, H L G V E J B H L G V E J B H L G V E J B H L G V E J B H L G V E J B

questions.question: Which two signs should be interchanged to make the below equation mathematically correct? 100 + 7 - 162 ÷ 18 X 36 = 127
In a certain code language, 'LOUD' is coded as '7596' and 'DONU' is coded as '9265'. What is the code for 'N' in the given code language?

­ Select the option that represents the letters which, when placed from left to right in the blanks below, will complete the letter series. M_O_N_ _TUS_UG_I_H_

So, option (a) is incorrect. Option (b) When the blanks are replaced sequentially by the given letters, then the series will be: MNOMNOSTUSTUGHIGHI Here, the pattern, MNO, MNO, STU, STU, GHI, GHI. Option (c) When the blanks are replaced sequentially by the given letters, then the series will be: MUOGNHITUSMUGNIOHS Here, no definite pattern can be established. So, option (c) is incorrect. Option (d) When the blanks are replaced sequentially by the given letters, then the series will be: MMONNHHTUSSUGTIOHM Here, no definite pattern can be established. So, option (d) is incorrect.

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'complete assign report' is written as 'one two three' and 'report is urgent' is written as 'three four five'. How will 'report' be coded?
questions.explanation: 'complete assign report' → one two three 'report is urgent' → three four five 'report' is common. So, code = three

questions.question: If 25 # 18 & 16 = 27 and 90 # 84 & 22 = 152, then the value of expression 24 # 8 & 10 is?
questions.explanation: Here # denotes '+' and & denotes '-'. Therefore, 24 + 8 - 10 = 32 - 10 = 22

questions.question: Find the odd from the following
questions.explanation: First letter is decreased by three from the second letter and third letter is increased by five from the second letter L-3= I; L+5= Q V-3= S; V+5= A W-3= T; W+5=B J-3= G; J+5= O

questions.question: Find the missing terms (i) and (ii) respectively in the series: S17K, _(i)_, Y23G, B29E, _(ii)_, H37A
questions.explanation: S + 3 = V, V + 3 = Y, Y + 3 = B, B + 3 = E, E + 3 = H K - 2 = I, I - 2 = G, G - 2 = E, E - 2 = C, C - 2 = A Consecutive primes are 17, 19, 23, 29, 31, 37 So, the missing terms respectively are: V19I and E31C

questions.question: Select the option in which the letter clusters share the same relationship as that shared by the given letter cluster pair. WB : HQ
questions.explanation: We have: ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 NOPQRSTUVWXYZ 14151617181920212223242526 W -6 = Q B + 6 = H The code is then reversed to get HQ. K - 6 = E F + 6 = L The code is then reversed to get LE

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Pulakeshin II: a. Pulakeshin II expanded the Chalukya kingdom into northern India. b. He defeated Harshavardhana and established the Narmada River as a key boundary. c. He is credited with the construction of the Brihadeshwara Temple at Thanjavur. Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: • Pulakeshin II famously defeated Harshavardhana and established the Narmada as a territorial boundary. • Pulakeshin II did expand southward but primarily focused on consolidating power in the Deccan rather than northern India. • The Brihadeshwara Temple was built by Rajaraja Chola I, not Pulakeshin II. Further Insight: • Chalukya Dynasty ○ Period: 6th - 8th century CE. ○ Key Ruler: ■ Pulakeshin II: Expanded the kingdom; defeated Harshavardhana, establishing the Narmada as a key boundary. ○ Capital: Badami. ○ Contributions: ■ Renowned for rock-cut and structural temples (e.g., Badami cave temples). ■ Patronage of Hinduism and Jainism, fostering regional art and architecture. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25852/image006.png)

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true? A. Mohiniyattam lays emphasis on acting. B. It has 24 hand gestures mainly adopted from Hastalakshana Deepika.
questions.explanation: Mohiniyattam lays emphasis on acting. The dancer identifies herself with the character and sentiments existing in the compositions like the Padams and Pada Varnams which give ample opportunity for facial expressions. The hand gestures, 24 in number, are mainly adopted from Hastalakshana Deepika, a text followed by Kathakali. Few are also borrowed from NatyaShastra, AbhinayaDarpana and Balarambharatam.

questions.question: In the following questions, a statement of Assertion (A) is followed by a statement of Reason (R). Mark the correct choice as: Assertion (A): A government may fail to reduce its total Fiscal Deficit even after successfully reducing its Primary Deficit. Reason (R): Interest payments on past borrowings are categorized as "committed expenditures" and depend on the level of past debt and interest rates.
options: Both (A) and (R) are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is True: Fiscal Deficit = Total Expenditure − Total Revenue (excluding borrowings) Primary Deficit = Fiscal Deficit − Interest Payments So even if the government reduces its Primary Deficit, the Fiscal Deficit may remain high if: Interest payments are large Interest burden increases Because Fiscal Deficit includes interest payments. Reason (R) is True: Interest payments are called committed expenditures because: Government cannot avoid paying them They arise from past borrowings They are influenced by interest rates in the economy If interest rates rise, interest burden can increase. (R) correctly explains (A): Since interest payments are fixed obligations and can increase with interest rate changes, they may offset reductions in primary deficit. So even if new spending is controlled, high interest payments can keep fiscal deficit high. Further Insights • If Primary Deficit = 0 → Fiscal Deficit = Interest Payments. • A high interest burden reduces money available for development spending. • Persistent fiscal deficit increases public debt. • Revenue deficit measures excess of revenue expenditure over revenue receipts.

questions.question: Consider the following statements in relation to a Money Bill and choose the correct code from the options given below. I- Money bill can be introduced only in the Lok Sabha. II- It can be detained by Rajya Sabha for a maximum period of 21 days only III- It requires a certification of the Lok Sabha Speaker when transmitted to the Rajya Sabha. IV- It can be introduced only by a minister.
questions.explanation: Let's analyze each statement based on the provisions of the Indian Constitution regarding a Money Bill (defined in Article 110 and procedures in Article 109). I- Money bill can be introduced only in the Lok Sabha. This statement is correct. According to Article 109(1) of the Constitution, a Money Bill cannot be introduced in the Rajya Sabha. It is an exclusive power of the Lok Sabha. II- It can be detained by Rajya Sabha for a maximum period of 21 days only. This statement is incorrect. According to Article 109(2), the Rajya Sabha must return a Money Bill to the Lok Sabha within a period of 14 days from the date of its receipt. If it fails to do so, the bill is deemed to have been passed by both Houses in the form it was passed by the Lok Sabha. The limit is 14 days, not 21. III- It requires a certification of the Lok Sabha Speaker when transmitted to the Rajya Sabha. This statement is correct. Article 110(4) mandates that when a Money Bill is sent to the Rajya Sabha, it must be endorsed with a certificate from the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, signed by him, stating that it is a Money Bill. The Speaker's decision on whether a bill is a Money Bill is final. IV- It can be introduced only by a minister. This statement is correct. A Money Bill is a type of government bill and can only be introduced by a minister. This is because it deals with government finances and requires the prior recommendation of the President for its introduction in the Lok Sabha (Article 117), a recommendation that is only available to the government.

questions.question: Which of the following international organizations did External Affairs Minister Dr. S. Jaishankar recently launch the 2026 Presidency logo and website in New Delhi?
questions.explanation: External Affairs Minister Dr. S. Jaishankar unveiled the logo, theme, and official website for India's BRICS Presidency 2026 in New Delhi. This marks India's chairship of the BRICS grouping for the year 2026. Further insights: · India will chair BRICS for the fourth time in 2026. · The theme for BRICS 2026 is focused on resilience, innovation, cooperation, and sustainability. · BRICS is a platform for emerging economies to coordinate on global issues. · The presidency of BRICS rotates annually among member countries.

questions.question: Consider the following statements with reference to 'Phad': A. It is a folk theatre of Rajasthan B. It is a folk painting of Rajasthan Choose the correct statements from the code given below:
options: Neither A nor B is true.
Both A and B are true.
questions.explanation: Shahpura in Bhilwara district of Rajasthan is known all over India because of its distinctive style of folk theatre and painting. Phad is recited by Rajputs, Gurjars, Jats, Kumbhkars and Bhopas of the Balai communities. Phad is a style of folk painting which is presented by theatre artists in the form of a colorful recital.They cover themselves with the Phad and travel from village to village. The unique art form is known for its vibrant colors, and the intricacy of storytelling- the painting itself and the accompanying musical folk performance.

questions.question: The Constitution (One Hundred and Thirtieth Amendment) Bill, 2025 was introduced in the Lok Sabha. It seeks to amend the Constitution to provide that a Prime Minister, Chief Minister, or other Minister will be removed from office if: A. they are accused of an offence punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to five years or more B. they are arrested and detained in custody for 60 consecutive days. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Constitution (One Hundred and Thirtieth Amendment) Bill, 2025 was introduced in Lok Sabha on August 20, 2025. It seeks to provide for removal of the Prime Minister, a Chief Minister of a state, or any other Minister in the central or a state government, if he is arrested and detained in custody on account of serious criminal offences. It also applies these provisions to the Union Territory (UT) of Delhi. Grounds for removal: A Minister will be removed from office if: (i) he is accused of an offence punishable with imprisonment for a term which may extend to five years or more, and (ii) he has been arrested and detained in custody for 30 consecutive days.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: Pond water and sea water are considered as fresh water.
Rivers and sea water are considered as fresh water.
Rivers, creeks, lakes, ponds, and streams are all freshwater habitats.
questions.explanation: The correct statement is (d) Rivers, creeks, lakes, ponds, and streams are all freshwater habitats. Freshwater habitats are aquatic ecosystems characterized by having a low concentration of dissolved salts (low salinity), typically less than 1%. All the water bodies listed in this optionrivers, creeks (small streams), lakes, ponds, and streams-fall under this definition. Explanation of Other Options: Pond water and sea water are considered as fresh water: This is incorrect because sea water is saline (saltwater), not freshwater. Sea water is freshwater habitat: This is incorrect. Sea water is the defining feature of a marine (saltwater) habitat. Rivers and sea water are considered as fresh water: This is incorrect because it wrongly classifies sea water as freshwater. Further insights: Aquatic ecosystems are broadly divided into two main types: freshwater ecosystems and marine (saltwater) ecosystems. Freshwater has a salinity of less than 0.5 parts per thousand (ppt). These ecosystems are crucial for biodiversity and provide most of the drinking water for humans. Marine water (e.g., oceans, seas) has a high salinity, averaging around 35 ppt. There is also an intermediate category called brackish water, where freshwater and saltwater mix, such as in estuaries. The salinity in brackish water is variable. Freshwater habitats support a wide variety of organisms, including fish like trout and bass, amphibians like frogs, various insects, and aquatic plants like water lilies.

questions.question: Identify which of the following statements is correct? A. Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (PMMY) was launched on April 8, 2015 B. These loans are given by Commercial Banks, RRBs, Small Finance Banks, MFIs and NBFCs. C. Under the aegis of PMMY, MUDRA has created three products namely 'Shishu', 'Kishore' and 'Tarun'
questions.explanation: · Pradhan Mantri MUDRA Yojana (PMMY) is a scheme launched by the Hon'ble Prime Minister on April 8, 2015 for providing loans up to 10 lakh to the non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. · These loans are classified as MUDRA loans under PMMY. · These loans are given by Commercial Banks, RRBs, Small Finance Banks, MFIs and NBFCs. · Under the aegis of PMMY, MUDRA has created three products namely 'Shishu', 'Kishore' and 'Tarun' to signify the stage of growth / development and funding needs of the beneficiary micro unit / entrepreneur and also provide a reference point for the next phase of graduation / growth. · Shishu- Covering Loans Upto 50,000/- · Kishore-Covering Loans Above 50,000/- And Upto 5 Lakh · Tarun- Covering Loans Above 5 Lakh And Upto 10 Lakh

questions.question: Match List-I (Committee) with List-II (Primary Purpose/Subject) and select the correct answer using the codes given below: List-I (Committee) List-II (Purpose) i) Balwant Rai Mehta Committee (1957)1. Reform of the Criminal Justice System ii) Santhanam Committee (1962)2. Three-tier Panchayati Raj System iii) Sarkaria Commission (1983)3. Prevention of Corruption (led to CVC) iv) V. V. Malimath Committee (2000)4. Centre-State Relations Codes:
questions.explanation: i → 2 (Balwant Rai Mehta Committee → Three-tier Panchayati Raj System) Recommended the Gram Panchayat - Panchayat Samiti - Zila Parishad structure to deepen democratic decentralisation. It designed India's basic local government system. ii → 3 (Santhanam Committee → Prevention of Corruption) Focused on curbing corruption; its recommendations led to setting up the Central Vigilance Commission (CVC). It proposed mechanisms to fight corruption. iii → 4 (Sarkaria Commission → Centre-State Relations) Examined federal relations and suggested measures for cooperative federalism. It studied how the Centre and States should work together. iv → 1 (V. V. Malimath Committee → Criminal Justice System Reform) Reviewed investigation, trials, and victims' rights to reform the criminal justice system. It suggested improvements to criminal law procedures.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about GDP and Welfare are correct? 1) GDP of a country is an imperfect measure of the welfare of the people. 2) If GDP is rising, welfare may not rise equally. 3) GDP and Welfare both are not related to each other.
questions.explanation: GDP of a country is an imperfect measure of the welfare of the people. This is correct. GDP measures economic output, but it fails to capture many crucial aspects of well-being. For example, it doesn't account for income inequality, the value of leisure time, unpaid work (like caring for family), environmental quality, or public health outcomes. Because of these limitations, it is widely acknowledged as an imperfect proxy for welfare. If GDP is rising, welfare may not rise equally. This is also correct. This statement directly follows from the first one. A rising GDP might be driven by factors that decrease welfare (e.g., producing goods that cause pollution) or the benefits of the growth might only go to a small segment of the population, leaving the majority's welfare unchanged or even worse off. Therefore, there is no guarantee that welfare will rise in proportion to GDP. GDP and Welfare both are not related to each other. This is incorrect. This is too strong a statement. While the relationship is imperfect, there is a strong positive correlation between GDP and many indicators of welfare. Generally, countries with higher GDP per capita have better healthcare, higher life expectancy, and higher levels of education. A higher GDP provides the resources necessary to improve these aspects of life. So, while not the same thing, they are certainly related. Hence, statements 1 and 2 are the correct ones.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of NavIC (IRNSS)?
options: Regional satellite navigation covering India and nearby region
Real-time cyclone modeling and weather radar
questions.explanation: NavIC provides position, navigation, and timing (PNT) services over India and around 1,500 km beyond. Its satellites are in GEO and inclined GSO orbits; new satellites add L1 band for wider device support. Further Insights: - Initial constellation size: 7 satellites (3 GEO, 4 GSO). - Services: Standard Positioning Service (SPS) for civilians; Restricted Service (RS) for authorized users. - NVS-01 (2023) began the second-generation upgrades to the constellation. - Uses include vehicle tracking, marine navigation, disaster management, and precise time transfer.

questions.question: Evaluate the following statements about the Vice President of India: a) The Vice President of India presides over the Lok Sabha sessions. b) The Vice President is elected for a term of 5 years. c) The Vice President acts as President in the absence of the President. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: • The Vice President presides over the Rajya Sabha (Article 64), not the Lok Sabha. • The Vice President is elected for a term of 5 years (Article 67). • The Vice President acts as President in the absence of the President (Article 65). Further Insight: • The Vice President is elected by Members of the Both Houses of Parliament(Article 66) while the President is elected by only elected members of Parliament and state legislative assembly(Article 54). • Vice President of India: • Article 63: There shall be a Vice-President of India. • Article 64: Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of Rajya Sabha. • Article 65: Acts as President in case of vacancy or absence. • Article 66: Elected by an electoral college of Parliament. • Article 67: Holds office for a five-year term. • Article 68: Election to be held before term ends or within six months of vacancy. • Article 69: Takes oath before the President or designated officer. • Article 70: Performs President's duties if both offices are vacant. • Article 71: Supreme Court handles election disputes of President or Vice-President.

questions.question: The Great Indian Novel, written by Shashi Tharoor in 1989, is best described as:
options: A historical biography of Indian leaders
A political satire retelling Indian history through the Mahabharata
A romantic novel set during the freedom struggle
questions.explanation: The Great Indian Novel is a satirical novel by Shashi Tharoor, first published by Viking Press in 1989. It is a fictional work that takes the story of the Mahabharata, the Indian epic, and recasts and resets it in the context of the Indian independence movement and the first three decades post-independence.

questions.question: Which State/UT won the first prize for the best tableau during the 77th Republic Day Parade held in 2026?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra won the first prize among States and Union Territories for its tableau titled "Ganeshotsav: A Symbol of Aatmanirbharta" during the 77th Republic Day Parade 2026. Jammu and Kashmir secured second place, while Kerala secured third place. Further insights: · Maharashtra's tableau theme was "Ganeshotsav: A Symbol of Aatmanirbharta." · Jammu and Kashmir came second with its handicrafts and folk dance theme. · Kerala came third for its Water Metro and digital literacy theme. · The Ministry of Culture won the best tableau award among central ministries.

questions.question: 'Margomkali', a ritual folk art form, is from the state of ______.
questions.explanation: 'Margomkali' is a ritual folk art form from the state of Kerala. It is traditionally performed by the Saint Thomas Christians (also known as Syrian Christians or Nasranis) of Kerala. The dance usually narrates the life and missionary work of St. Thomas the Apostle, who is believed to have brought Christianity to India. Explanation of Other Options: (a) Assam: Known for folk dances like Bihu and the classical dance Sattriya. (b) Punjab: Known for folk dances like Bhangra and Giddha. (c) Nagaland: Known for various tribal war dances and festive dances. Further insights: Margomkali is typically performed by women in a group, dancing in a circle around a lighted lamp (nilavilakku). The performance involves simple, graceful dance steps accompanied by singing. The songs and narrative of Margomkali are based on apocryphal texts and traditions related to the life of St. Thomas. It is performed during religious festivals, church functions, and other community celebrations. The costume is the traditional white attire of Syrian Christian women, often the 'Chattayum Mundum'.

questions.question: In Census 2011, literacy rate is calculated for people aged at least how many completed years?
questions.explanation: Census 2011 treats a person as literate if aged 7 years and above and able to read and write with understanding in any language. Hence the literacy rate is computed for the population aged 7+. Further Insights: - Overall literacy (2011): 74.04% (male 82.14%, female 65.46%). - Kerala (state) had the highest literacy; Bihar the lowest among states. - Rural literacy (2011): 67.77%; urban literacy: 84.11%. - "Effective literacy rate" excludes 0 - 6 population from the denominator.

questions.question: The security mission of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) primarily targets the "Three Evils," which are:
questions.explanation: The SCO defines the "Three Evils" as: Terrorism Separatism Extremism These are considered the main threats to regional peace and stability in Eurasia. The SCO was formed mainly to promote security cooperation among member countries, especially against militant movements and separatist activities. Further Insights • SCO was founded in 2001. • India and Pakistan became full members in 2017. • The Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure (RATS) coordinates anti-terror efforts. • SCO focuses mainly on Eurasian regional security. • China and Russia are key founding members.

questions.question: The phenomenon of temperature inversion is most likely to occur:
questions.explanation: Under normal atmospheric conditions, the air temperature decreases as you go higher (an "environmental lapse rate"). However, during a temperature inversion, this pattern is reversed: a layer of warm air sits on top of a layer of cooler air near the earth's surface. Why it occurs during clear winter nights For a "Surface Inversion" to occur, the ground must cool down rapidly. This happens best under the following conditions: Long Winter Nights: The earth has more time to radiate its heat away into space (terrestrial radiation) than it has to absorb heat from the sun. Clear Skies: Clouds act like a blanket, trapping heat near the surface. Without clouds, heat escapes easily into the upper atmosphere. Calm Air: Strong winds would mix the warm and cold layers. Still air allows the cold air (which is denser) to settle and remain trapped at the surface. Dry Air: Moisture can absorb and hold heat; dry air allows for more rapid cooling.

questions.question: Consider the statements regarding new criminal law codes notified in 2023. I. Bharatiya Nagrik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 (BNSS) largely replaces the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973. II. Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam, 2023 (BSA) repeals the Indian Evidence Act, 1872. III. These three new laws are constitutional amendments under Article 368. Which of the above statement is/are correct?
questions.explanation: I is correct. BNSS supersedes CrPC procedures. II is correct. BSA replaces the 1872 Evidence Act. III is incorrect. These are ordinary laws passed by Parliament, not constitutional amendments. Further Insights: - Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (BNS) replaces the Indian Penal Code, 1860. - The Acts commenced in 2024 (with notified dates), modernizing criminal law procedures. - Provisions include timelines for investigation/trial and technology-enabled processes.

questions.question: The Swaraj Party was formed after the withdrawal of which of the following movements?
questions.explanation: · The Swaraj Party was formed in 1923 after the withdrawal of the Non-Cooperation Movement (1922). · Leaders like C. R. Das and Motilal Nehru believed in entering legislative councils to: o Oppose colonial policies from within o Expose the limitations of British rule Civil Disobedience Movement - Launched in 1930. Quit India Movement - Launched in 1942 Home Rule Movement - Started in 1916. Further insights · Swaraj Party advocated the strategy of Council Entry. · It represented a split between No-Changers and Pro-Changers in the Congress. · The party aimed at achieving self-government within the British framework. · It played a significant role in Central Legislative Assembly debates.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding 'The Smart Sea Project by VACE' signed in December 2025: Statement I: The project involves a partnership between India and Canada to develop and demonstrate a 20-passenger electric boat. Statement II: The private Indian partner for this sustainable maritime initiative is Vijai Marine Services Pvt. Ltd., based in Goa. Which of the following is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement I is correct The Smart Sea Project by VACE is part of an Indo-Canada Collaborative Industrial R&D Programme. It aims to develop and demonstrate a 20-passenger electric boat to promote clean and sustainable waterways. Statement II is correct The private Indian partner in this project is Vijai Marine Services Pvt. Ltd. (VMSPL), a Goa-based marine company responsible for building the vessel. Further insights: · The project is supported by the Technology Development Board (TDB) under DST. · Canadian partners include NRC and Global Affairs Canada. · The boat will use electric propulsion and battery systems. · It aims to reduce emissions and noise in waterways. · It supports sustainable tourism and green transport.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a direct tax?
questions.explanation: · Direct tax is a tax that is imposed and collected by the government from an individual or organization. It cannot be shifted to another individual or entity. · It includes personal income tax, corporate tax, property tax, gift tax, capital gains tax etc. · Goods and Services Tax (GST) is an indirect tax. · The GST subsumed all previous taxes that were levied on the sale of goods or provision of services by either Central or State Government.

questions.question: Identify the correct statement(s). Statement I: In Arunachal Pradesh, the river is known as Siang before it meets the Dibang and the Lohit near Sadiya to form the Brahmaputra. Statement II: Majuli, one of the largest river islands, lies within the Brahmaputra system near Jorhat.
options: Both I and II are correct.
Neither I nor II is correct.
questions.explanation: The Tsangpo becomes Siang (Dihang) in Arunachal; it joins with the Dibang and Lohit near Sadiya and then is called Brahmaputra in Assam. Majuli lies in the Brahmaputra with channels and the Subansiri influencing its boundaries. Further Insights: - The river is called YarlungTsangpo in Tibet, Siang/Dihang in Arunachal, and Jamuna in Bangladesh. - The Brahmaputra is highly braided with frequent channel shifts. - Riverine islands like Majuli are vulnerable to erosion and avulsion.

questions.question: Consider the pair of statements regarding Porifera (Sponges): Statement I: Sponges show cellular level of organization and lack true tissues and organs. Statement II: Many sponges reproduce asexually by budding; freshwater sponges form gemmules as resistant structures. Which of the above statement is/are true?
questions.explanation: Sponges are at the cellular level of organization and do not have true tissues. They indeed reproduce asexually by budding, and freshwater forms produce gemmules; however, this does not explain the absence of tissues. Further Insights: - Choanocytes (collar cells) drive water flow and capture food. - Skeleton made of spicules (calcium carbonate/silica) or spongin fibers. - Asconoid, syconoid, leuconoid are canal system types. - Spongilla is a common freshwater sponge; Euplectella (glass sponge) shows silica spicules.

questions.question: A vendor buys mangoes at 15 for Rs. 210 and sells them at 14 for Rs. 238. What is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Profit% = [(Selling price − Cost price) / Cost price] X 100 Cost price of 1 mango = 210/15 = Rs. 14 Selling price of 1 mango = 238/14 = Rs. 17 Profit per mango = 17 − 14 = Rs. 3 Profit% = (3/14) X 100 = 300/14% = 150/7% = 21(3/7)%

questions.question: A tank contains 100 litres of a solution in which acid is 80% by volume. If 20 litres of the solution are removed and replaced with 20 litres of pure water, what will be the percentage of acid in the final mixture?
questions.explanation: Initial acid = 100 X (80/100) = 80 litres Acid removed = 20 X (80/100) = 16 litres Acid remaining = 80 − 16 = 64 litres New total volume = 100 litres New percentage of acid = (64/100) X 100 = 64%

questions.question: The lateral surface area of a pyramid whose base is a regular pentagon is 240 cm2. The length of each side of the pentagon is 6 cm. Find the slant height of the pyramid.
questions.explanation: LSA = 1/2 * (perimeter) * slant 1/2 * 5 * 6 * l = 240 15l = 240 => l = 16 cm.

questions.question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are TRUE ? I. √5 + √5 > √7 + √3 II. √6 + √7 > √8 + √5 III. √3 + √9 > √6 + √6

Squaring both sides and then add ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/stubeimg/SSCT-MML-004096.png) (False) Hence, only I and II are true

questions.question: The average monthly income of employees in a company is ₹72,000. When a new employee with income ₹1,08,000 joins, the average increases by ₹1,500. How many employees were originally working in the company?
questions.explanation: Let original number of employees = n Total original income = 72000n New average = 72000 + 1500 = 73500 New total income = 73500(n + 1) 72000n + 108000 = 73500(n + 1) 72000n + 108000 = 73500n + 73500 108000 − 73500 = 73500n − 72000n 34500 = 1500n n = 23

questions.question: If A = 3 x t - 1_________5 and B = 2t + 3, then for what value of t is A = B.
questions.explanation: According to the question, A = B 3 * (t - 1)/5 = 2t + 3 3t - 3 = 10t + 15 7t = -18 t = -18/7

questions.question: Simplify: (cos11° + sin11°)/(cos11° - sin11°)
If Nitu's salary is 45% more than Naina's salary, then by what percent is Naina's salary less than Nitu's salary (Approx)?
questions.explanation: Let Naina's salary be Rs. 100. Nitu's salary = 100 * 145/100 Hence, the required percentage = (145 - 100) * 100/145 = 31.04%

questions.question: What is the equation of the line having slope 4 and passing through the point (-2, 3)?
questions.explanation: Equation of a line passing through the point (x1, y1) with slope m => y - y1 = m(x - x1) Here, x1 = -2, y1 = 3, m = 4 => y - 3 = 4{x - (-2)} => y - 3 = 4(x + 2) => y - 3 = 4x + 8 => y = 4x + 11

questions.question: A garment store purchased 4,000 T-shirts at a total cost of ₹8,00,000. They gave away 400 T-shirts for a promotional campaign. For the remaining T-shirts, they offered a 25% discount on the marked price of ₹600 per T-shirt. Additionally, they offered a scheme of Buy 4 T-shirts, Get 1 Free. If all 4,000 T-shirts were distributed, what is the overall gain or loss percentage for the store?

Pipe A can fill a cistern in 20 hours, pipe B can fill it in 30 hours, and pipe C alone can empty it in 60 hours. If all three pipes are opened together, how many hours will they take to fill 70% of the tank?
questions.explanation: Let total tank = 1 unit. Rate of A = 1/20 per hour Rate of B = 1/30 per hour Rate of C = - 1/60 per hour Net rate = 1/20 + 1/30 - 1/60 = (3 + 2 - 1)/60 = 4/60 = 1/15 per hour Time to fill 70% = 0.70 ÷ (1/15) = 0.70 X 15 = 10.5 hours

questions.question: Two investment plans are offered on the same principal P. Plan A gives simple interest at 15% per annum for 2 years, while Plan B gives compound interest at 15% per annum compounded annually for 2 years. If Plan B yields Rs. 108 more than Plan A, find P.
questions.explanation: Difference between compound and simple interest for the same principal for 2 years = P(R/100)2 P(15/100)2 = 108 P = 108*400/9 P = 12*400 P = ₹4800

questions.question: Three types of wooden sticks of length 150 cm, 180 cm and 120 cm respectively are joined together to form three large sticks such that length of each large stick is equal and only sticks of similar length are used to form large stick, then find the maximum number of small sticks used to form large sticks.
questions.explanation: Since number of small sticks are minimum. So, length of each large stick should be as large as possible. Maximum possible length of each large stick = LCM of 150, 180 and 120 = 1800 cm Length of each large stick = 1800 cm Total sticks of 150 cm length needed = 1800/150 = 12 Total sticks of 180 cm length needed = 1800/180 = 10 Total sticks of 120 cm length needed = 1800/120 = 15 Required number o sticks = 12 + 10 + 15 = 37

questions.question: A sum becomes 9 times its original value in 10 years at compound interest, compounded annually. In how many years from the beginning will it become 243 times the original sum?
questions.explanation: Amount under compound interest = Principal X (1 + rate/100)time Given, (1 + r/100)10 = 9 Let the required time be t years. Then, (1 + r/100)t = 243 = 35 => 243 = 95/2 => (1 + r/100)t = {(1 + r/100)10}5/2 => (1 + r/100)t = (1 + r/100)25 => t = 25

questions.question: Altitude KN of a ∆KLM is 4.5 cm and the area of triangles ∆KLM, and ∆PQR are 324 cm2 and 169 cm2. If ∆KLM is similar to ∆PQR, then the altitude PS of ∆PQR is equal to:
questions.explanation: Given ∆KLM ~ ∆PQR So, KN/PS = √324/√169 => PS = 4.5 * 13/18 => PS = 3.25 cm

questions.question: What is the simplified value of sec2x (sin3x - sin x)?
questions.explanation: sec2x (sin3x - sin x) = sec2x sin3x - sec2x sin x = sin x (sin2x/cos2x) − sec2x sin x = sin x tan2x - sin x sec2x = sin x (tan2x - sec2x) = − sin x

questions.question: A solid metal sphere of radius 6 cm is melted and recast into smaller solid spheres, each of radius 2 cm. How many such smaller spheres are formed?
questions.explanation: Number of smaller spheres = volume of original sphere / volume of one smaller sphere Number of smaller spheres = { (4/3) X π X 63 } / { (4/3) X π X 23} = 63 / 23 = 216 / 8 = 27 Hence, 27 smaller spheres are formed.

questions.question: The average of 25 observations is 68. If one observation is excluded, the average of the remaining 24 observations becomes 65. What is the excluded observation?
questions.explanation: Average = (sum of observations) / (number of observations) Total of 25 observations = 25 X 68 = 1700 Total of remaining 24 observations = 24 X 65 = 1560 Excluded observation = 1700 − 1560 = 140

questions.question: A conical flask is perfectly fitted inside a square shaped container. Base diameter of flask and side of container is 15 cm each and height of flask is also 15 cm. What is the ratio of the cone's volume to that of the square shaped container?
questions.explanation: Radius of conical flask = 15/2 cm Height of flask = 15 cm Side of square shaped container = 15 cm Required ratio = volume of the cone / volume of square shaped container = (1/3 * 22/7 * 15/2 * 15/2 * 15) / (15 * 15 * 15) = 11/42

questions.question: If the perimeter of a right-angled isosceles triangle is 6(√2 + 1) cm, then the length of the hypotenuse is:
questions.explanation: Perimeter of isosceles right-angled triangle = Hypotenuse (√2 + 1) Given, 6 * (√2 + 1) = Hypotenuse (√2 + 1) So, hypotenuse = 6 cm

questions.question: The price of petrol is increased by 50%. How much percent must a car owner reduce his consumption of petrol so as not to increase his expenditure on petrol?
questions.explanation: Logic: expenditure constant => price X consumption constant. Let original consumption = 100 L New price = 150% of original New consumption = 100 / 1.50 = 200/3 = 66(2/3) L Reduction = (100 − 200/3)/100 X 100% = 100/3 % = 33(1/3)%

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the boldened idiom. She knew it was time to face the music and accept responsibility for her actions.
questions.explanation: The idiom "face the music" means to accept the unpleasant consequence/reality. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech. The teacher said that Caesar conquered many nations.
options: "Caesar conquers many nations", said the teacher.
"Caesar has conquered many nations", said the teacher.
"Caesar conquered many nations", said the teacher.
"Many nations are conquered by Caesar", said the teacher.
questions.explanation: If the sentence is in Simple Past, the form of tense doesn't change when it expresses a historical past. For example: The teacher said that Mahatma Gandhi was born in Gujarat. (Indirect Speech) The teacher said, "Mahatma Gandhi was born in Gujarat." (Direct Speech) The following things in the sentence will change when changed from Indirect Speech to Direct Speech: · 'That' is removed and inverted commas ("...") are added. The tense of all options except (c) has been changed. The tense for option (d) is also changed, i.e., to present tense (in passive voice: is/are + V3). So, option (d) is incorrect. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer. Therefore, the correct answer is: "Caesar conquered many nations", said the teacher.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in a sentence. Mark is a gregarious person who always enjoys socialising at parties.
questions.explanation: Gregarious: Fond of company; sociable; enjoying the company of others. Solitary: Done or existing alone; without companions. Affable: Friendly, good-natured, and easy to talk to. Outgoing: Friendly and socially confident; extroverted. Convivial: Friendly, sociable, and fond of good company. Among the given options, the word 'solitary' is opposite in meaning to the word 'gregarious'. So, the two words can be used as antonyms of each other. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Rearrange the following stages in the formation of a public policy in the correct order: P. Legislators debate the proposed bill and may suggest amendments after committee review. Q. A policy issue is identified and placed on the government's formal agenda. R. The executive signs the approved bill into law, making it an official public policy. S. Government analysts draft a detailed bill outlining goals and implementation mechanisms.
questions.explanation: The sequence reflects the standard steps in the policy-making cycle. Q is the first step, where a societal problem is formally recognized. S follows, when experts translate the agenda item into a concrete bill draft. P comes next, when lawmakers debate and refine the drafted legislation. R is the concluding step, where the executive approves the bill and enacts it into law. Thus, the correct order is Q → S → P → R. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. The Prime Minister, who was the only person admitted, felt it his duty at last to tell the king how much the court and all the people complained of his seclusion. Q. He urged the sultan to move with the prince to a lovely little island close by. R. Here he could easily attend public audiences. S. Also, the charming scenery and fine air would do the king much good.
questions.explanation: P begins the passage by telling us about the main topic - the people complaining about the king's seclusion. Q follows by telling us what the prime minister urged the king to do to solve this problem. RS follow as a pair: R tells us how this solution (mentioned in P) would benefit the nation. S concludes by telling us how it would benefit the king. PQRS is the final order.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the given four alternatives, select the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the Idiom/Phrase. Dance with death
options: to be thought to be dead
to do something involving a lot of risks
questions.explanation: The idiom 'dance with death' means to undertake or do something that involves a lot of risks. E.g. He danced with death when he challenged Raj to a wrestling match, as Raj is twice his size. Hence, (d) is the right answer.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: The word given in option A is spelt incorrectly. The correct spelling is 'benevolent'. It means kind and helpful. Option A is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. The assembling of car parts was being done in this section.
options: This section was doing the assembling of car parts.
This section is doing the assembling of car parts
This section will be doing the assembling of car parts
This section has done the assembling of car parts.
questions.explanation: The changes that happen while converting Passive voice to Active voice sentence are: the Subject in the Passive voice becomes the Object in the Active voice and the Object in the Passive voice will become the Subject in the Active voice. The helping verb ['being' here] should be removed while changing from Passive voice to Active voice. The tense of the sentence in Passive voice remains the same in the Active voice too. In this Passive voice sentence, the tense is Past Continuous tense. The Passive voice format is Object of the Active voice (Subjective case) + was/were + being + V3 + by + Subject of the Active voice (Objective case). The Active voice format is Subject + was/were + Verb (ing) + Object. For example: The blood sample was being collected in this lab. (Passive voice) This lab was collecting the blood sample. (Active voice) From the given options, the one that follows the above-mentioned changes is option (a), "This section was doing the assembling of car parts". Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: CONTRITE
questions.explanation: Contrite - feeling or expressing remorse or penitence; affected by guilt. Remorseful - feeling deep regret or guilt for a wrong committed. Both words describe the feeling of being very sorry and guilty for a wrongdoing. Hence, option c) is the correct answer. Unrepentant - showing no regret for one's wrongdoings. Confident - feeling or showing certainty about something; self-assured. Trite - (of a remark or idea) lacking originality or freshness; overused.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word. The jury found the evidence insufficient and decided to acquit the defendant of all charges.
questions.explanation: Acquit - free (someone) from a criminal charge by a verdict of not guilty. Convict - declare (someone) to be guilty of a criminal offence by the verdict of a jury or the decision of a judge in a court of law. Both words have opposite meanings. So, "convict" is the required antonym. Hence, option (a) is the answer. Discharge - tell (someone) officially that they can or must leave a place or situation. Perform - carry out, accomplish, or fulfil (an action, task, or function). Vindicate - clear (someone) of blame or suspicion.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate word to fill in the blank. The case was deemed to be __________ as there were no prime witnesses to it.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, "abortive" fits the context of the sentence. It gives us the idea that because there were no key witnesses, the lawsuit was considered futile. Abortive: failing to produce the intended result; futile. The word that must fit the blank should be an adjective as it is describing the 'case' (noun). 'Futility' is a noun and thus, it is an incorrect option. 'Pacifying' is a contextual misfit as per its meaning. 'Successful' is a contextual misfit as the absence of prime witnesses hints at the futility of the case. Thus, the correct option is (a), abortive. Thus, the correct sentence should be: The case was deemed to be abortive as there were no prime witnesses to it. Additional Information: The respective meanings of the other options are given below in the table: WordMeaning Futilitypointlessness or uselessness Successfulaccomplishing a desired aim or result Pacifyingquell the anger, agitation, or excitement of

questions.question: Select the most suitable word to fill the given blank in the following sentence. The officer is to be court-martialled _____ his arrival.
questions.explanation: The blank requires a preposition that connects the acts of court-martial and the arrival. 'Upon' (more formal term for on, at the time of) is the most appropriate preposition that denotes the time at which the act of court-martial is to happen and can fill the blank. 'In' (expressing the situation of something that is or appears to be enclosed or surrounded by something else), 'onto' (moving to a location on the surface of) and 'to' (identifying the person or thing affected by or receiving something) are either grammatically or contextually unfit for the sentence and thus negated. The complete sentence is: The officer is to be court-martialled upon his arrival. Hence, the correct answer is option (a). Note: A Preposition is a word placed before a noun or a pronoun or a noun equivalent (pronoun, adverb, gerund, infinitive, adverbial clause, or any clause that can be used as the object of the preposition) and shows a relationship in space or time or a logical relationship between two or more people, places, or things in a sentence. Examples I. The teacher scolded the child for being late. II. We're going to a party. · The words in bold are called prepositions because they normally take position before (pre) a noun/pronoun or a noun equivalent (pronoun, adverb, gerund, infinitive, adverbial clause, or any clause that can be used as the object of the preposition. · The noun/pronoun which is used with a preposition is called its Object.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word. The young knight's impetuous actions in battle, though brave, often lacked careful strategy.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate synonym for "impetuous" is 'impulsive'. "Impetuous" describes actions or decisions that are made suddenly, without careful thought or consideration, which aligns with the idea of impulsiveness. 'Impulsive' means acting or deciding suddenly, without careful thought or consideration. Abstract: Separate from concrete reality, often theoretical or conceptual. Reticent: Unwilling to speak or communicate openly. Timid: Lacking courage or confidence. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses a homonym of the underlined word: She pinned the notice on the board.
options: The teacher wrote the schedule on the classroom board.
He hung the map on the notice board.
They used the board of a cork to display student projects.
The conductor asked the passengers to board the bus quickly.
questions.explanation: Homonyms are words that are spelled the same and pronounced the same but have different meanings. In the original sentence, "board" is a noun meaning a flat piece of wood or surface used to pin notices. Options a), b), and c) also refer to the same type of board, so their meanings are similar. Option d) uses "board" as a verb meaning to get on or enter a vehicle, which is completely unrelated to the flat surface meaning. This makes it a homonym. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Find the part of the sentence that contains an error: The investigators found that the suspect's alibi (1)/ did not correspond with the timeline (2)/ provided by the surveillance footage (3)/ and neither does his statements to the police. (4)
options: did not correspond with the timeline (2)
The investigators found that the suspect's alibi (1)
and neither does his statements to the police (4)
questions.explanation: Part (4) contains two issues: Subject - verb agreement: "statements" is plural, so the verb must be plural "do", not "does." Tense consistency: The earlier part uses past ("did not correspond"), so this clause must also use past "did", not "does." Correct: "..and neither did his statements to the police." Hence, part (4) is incorrect. Note: When using neither, the auxiliary verb must match both tense and number.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of alliteration?
options: The cat sat on the mat.
The rain in Spain stays mainly in the plain.
Time flies when you're having fun.
Sally sells seashells by the seashore.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (d). Alliteration repeats the same initial consonant sounds in a series of words (here, the "s" sound) to create a rhythmic, memorable effect, often used in tongue twisters. The cat sat on the mat is incorrect because the "t" sounds are coincidental and sparse, not a deliberate pattern for stylistic emphasis. Time flies when you're having fun is incorrect as it's an idiom with figurative meaning, focusing on concept rather than sound repetition. The rain in Spain stays mainly in the plain is incorrect since it features assonance (repeated "ai" vowel sounds), not initial consonant repetition. Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. When a rainy day keeps / me indoors, / I amuse mine after / the manner of other girls.
questions.explanation: A reflexive pronoun is used after 'acquit, avail, amuse, reconcile, amuse, resign, avenge, exert, apply, adapt, adjust, pride, absent and enjoy.' So, the use of 'amuse myself' will be correct here in place of 'amuse mine' in which 'mine' is not a reflexive pronoun but a possessive pronoun. Hence, option (c) is correct. NOTE/- Reflexive Pronouns: Reflexive pronouns include myself, himself, herself, itself, ourselves, yourselves, and themselves. These are used to refer back to the subject of the clause in which they are used: She hurt herself in the eye. Possessive Pronouns: The pronouns mine, yours, hers, his, ours, and theirs are known as possessive pronouns: they refer to something owned by the speaker or by someone or something previously mentioned. For example: The pen is mine.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person who is rough and bad-mannered.
questions.explanation: Let's look at the meanings of the given words. Agnostic: a person who believes that nothing is known or can be known of the existence or nature of God. Arsonist: a person who deliberately sets fire to a building or vehicle. Boorish: rough and bad-mannered; coarse. Egotist: a person who is excessively conceited or absorbed in himself/herself; self-seeker. Among the given options, 'boorish' is the correct choice. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The overall tone of the passage can best be described as:
options: Analytical and reflective about the shift towards realistic filmmaking.
Emotional and nostalgic about traditional storytelling methods.
Critical and dismissive of modern audience expectations.
Humorous and satirical about changes in the film industry.
questions.explanation: The passage explains how filmmaking has evolved from simple storytelling to more realistic and research-based narratives, highlighting changes in audience expectations and industry practices in a logical and balanced manner. For instance, it states, "This shift has significantly expanded the creative ecosystem of filmmaking." (Paragraph 1) and "the film industry's evolution reflects a broader cultural shift towards realism and accountability." (Paragraph 3). This indicates a reasoned and reflective analysis rather than emotional or critical commentary. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer. Option (b) is incorrect because the passage does not express nostalgia. Option (c) is incorrect because the tone is not dismissive. Option (d) is incorrect because no humour or satire is used.

questions.question: Which of the following best represents the main argument of the passage?
options: Realistic and research-based storytelling has become essential for success in modern filmmaking.
Traditional storytelling with versatile techniques are still relevant in the film industry.
Actors alone are responsible for the success of modern films.
Audiences prefer fictional content over fact-based narratives.
questions.explanation: The passage emphasizes that modern audiences demand authenticity and factual accuracy, making research and realism crucial in filmmaking. It states, "What truly captivates viewers now is compelling and realistic storytelling.." (Paragraph 1) and concludes that "Success now depends not just on creativity but on credibility." (Paragraph 3). This clearly establishes that realistic and research-based storytelling is central to modern filmmaking success. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: According to the passage, why are research analysts increasingly important in filmmaking?
options: They ensure that the storyline is accurate and grounded in detail.
They help actors memorise dialogues more effectively.
They replace the role of screenplay writers in film production.
They focus primarily on marketing and audience outreach.
questions.explanation: The passage clearly states, "the inclusion of research analysts has become equally crucial. These professionals ensure that the storyline is grounded in reality, supported by accurate details.." (Paragraph 1). This shows that their role is to enhance factual accuracy and realism in films. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The word "dissemination" in the passage most nearly means:
options: Creation of fictional narratives for entertainment.
Spread or distribution of information to people.
Analysis of audience reactions and preferences.
Restriction of content to avoid criticism.
questions.explanation: The passage states, "It also prevents the dissemination of misinformation.." (Paragraph 2). In this context, dissemination refers to the spreading or distribution of information, particularly incorrect information. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Identify the type of adverb used in the word "instantly" in the sentence: "With easy access to information, viewers can instantly fact-check and evaluate what they see on screen."
options: Adverb of manner describing how an action is performed.
Adverb of time indicating when an action takes place.
Adverb of place indicating where an action occurs.
Adverb of frequency indicating how often an action occurs.
questions.explanation: The sentence is: "With easy access to information, viewers can instantly fact-check and evaluate what they see on screen." The word "instantly" is the adverb in focus. Step 1: Determine the meaning of "instantly" "Instantly" means "immediately" or "at once". It tells us when the viewers can fact-check - right away, without delay. This is why it is the correct answer. Adverb of manner (describing how an action is performed) o Examples: quickly, carefully, silently. o These describe how the action happens. Adverb of place (indicating where an action occurs) o Examples: here, there, everywhere. o These tell where the action happens. Adverb of frequency (indicating how often an action occurs) o Examples: always, often, rarely. o These tell how often something happens. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Willing to give in to the wishes of another.
questions.explanation: 'Amenable' means willing to give in to the wishes of another. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. 'Amiable' means having or displaying a friendly and pleasant manner. 'Amorphous' means without a clearly defined shape or form. 'Analogy' means a comparison between two things, typically for the purpose of explanation or clarification.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The girl did not intending of stealing the book.
questions.explanation: Option C is the right answer. Option A is eliminated, as the sentence needs improvement. Option B is eliminated since this sentence structure makes the sentence sound incongruous and grammatically incorrect. Option D is eliminated since it would only be correct if the preposition 'of' would not have been used in the sentence right after the underlined part.
